svg.text: Terrain Editing
Create, modify, and manage terrain within a stage using the Terrain Editing system.
I. Overview
Entry Point
Access Terrain Editing from the first button in the top tab bar; click to toggle it.
Initial Creation
When a stage saving is created, a 100 × 100 Terrain is generated automatically at world coordinates (0, 0, 0).
Operating Units
The smallest operable unit is a single-layer area (size 5 × 5; layer height 2.5).
II. Main Interface
Assets Bar
Open: Use the upper-left button to toggle open and close.
Function: Select assets to create.
Asset Types
- Area Material (click to enter Free Edit mode)
- Custom Prefab (click to create)
- Prefabricated Components (click to create)
- Asset Library
Details Panel
Open: Use the upper-right button to toggle open and close.
Function: Manage terrains within stages.
Terrain List
- Rename: Double-click name to modify the selected Terrain name.
- Focus: Click button to position the camera at the terrain.
- Create Terrain: Click button to enter Terrain Creation mode.
Terrain Details (single selection only)
- Modify Position (Input): X value, Y value, Z value
- Modify Rotation (Input): Y value
- Area Material: Click button to open Replace panel. Replace or remove material.
Note: Clicking the button clears all areas of that material.
Pointer
Opening: Available only when Edit Mode is not active.
Function: Select one or more items (same logic as object selection).
Toolbars change based on the current selection.
Selectable Content and Selection Functions
- Terrain (detailed functionality in Terrain Modification)
- Static Objects, Dynamic Objects, Creations (modify position or open editor)
III. Terrain Creation
How to Enable
Left Assets Bar:
After entering Terrain Modification, click the [Created Based on Grid Lines] button
in the lower right corner.
Right Details Panel:
From the Main Interface: Details Panel > Terrain List > Create Terrain.
Click End Drawing (X button, top-right corner) to generate terrain.
Creation Mode

text.tspan: With the selected material in the Material Panel, the canvas height set by the Height Scale,
and the brush mode chosen in the Bottom Toolbar, use the brush at the current position

svg.text: Generation Logic
All connected areas form a single terrain; disconnected areas create multiple terrains.
Available Functions
(1) Material Panel
Switch Brush Material: Click to choose brush terrain material (7 types).
(2) Height Scale
- Move slider to change the canvas height.
- Click the slider to enter a value and change the canvas height.
- Focus on the canvas height.
- Increase or decrease canvas height by 20.
(3) Bottom Toolbar - Brush Mode Switcher
Area Actions
- Brush Layers: Set the layer count per stroke.
- Create: Raise the selected area by x layers.
- Delete: Lower the selected area by x layers.
- Level Out: Set the selected area to the specified height.
Note: After editing, the entire area stack adopts the current brush material.
Slope Actions (existing areas only)
- Set Slope Direction: Forward / Left / Right
- Slope Material: Varies depending on the area material.
- Create Slope: Add one slope layer on top of the selected area;
the higher end points in the chosen direction.
- Delete Slope: Remove the selected slope.
Water Body Actions (existing areas or water bodies only)
- Create Water Body: Convert top layer of selected area to water.
- Remove Water Body: Convert top water layer back to area.
Path Operation (existing areas only)
- Create Path: Draw a path on the top surface of the selected area.
- Delete Path: Remove the path from the top surface of the selected area.
(4) Brush Actions
- Tap: Apply the configured brush effect once at the current position.
- Drag: Apply the configured brush effect at each valid cursor position.
(5) End Drawing
Generate terrains from the content created on the canvas.
Click End Drawing (X button, top-right corner) to finalize.
IV. Terrain Modification - Pointer Mode
How to Enable
Available only when Edit Mode is not active (Main Terrain Editing interface).
(1) Single Terrain Selection
Transform Tools
- Motion: Change the terrain's position (X, Y, Z).
- Rotation: Change the terrain's rotation (Y value).
- Switching: Press Space to cycle between Motion, Rotation, and Mixed.
Layer Adjustment
- Raise a Layer: Raise all areas in selected terrain by one layer.
- Lower a Layer: Lower all areas in selected terrain by one layer.
Area Selection
Click to enter Precise Editing mode. Use pointer to select areas;
hold Shift and left-click to add them to the selection.
- Raise: Raise selected areas by one layer.
- Lower: Lower selected areas by one layer.
- Split: Detach selected areas and generate one or more new terrains.
Precise Editing
Enter Precise Editing mode for the selected Terrain via the bottom toolbar.
Delete Terrain
Delete all selected terrains.
Note: The bottom toolbar updates based on the current selection.
(2) Multiple Terrain Selection
Act on all selected terrains simultaneously.
Available Functions
- Transform Tools (Motion and Rotation)
- Delete
(3) Right-click Menu
Copy, Paste, Camera Focus, Delete, Assets Exporting, Character Dummy,
Add to Entity Deployment Group
V. Terrain Modification - Free Edit
How to Enable
On the Main Terrain Editing interface (not in any Edit Mode), click any material
button in the Material Panel.
How It Works

text.tspan: Cursor starts in pointer mode for terrain selection. Click to select terrain, then cursor
switches to brush mode. Paint areas with the chosen material and brush mode.
Clicking another terrain ends drawing, generates terrains, returns to selection.

svg.text: Available Functions (matches Scene Creation except no Height Scale)
Material Panel: Change brush material; replace terrain material.
Bottom Functional Area: Area Actions, Water Body Actions, Path Operation.
VI. Terrain Modification - Precise Editing
How to Enable
In Pointer mode, select a terrain and use the bottom toolbar to enter Precise Editing
for the selection.
How It Works

text.tspan: You can edit only the terrain selected before entering. Cursor switches to brush mode.
Using the material from the Material Panel and the brush mode from the Bottom Toolbar,
paint to create areas. Click End Drawing to generate terrains.

svg.text: Available Functions (matches Scene Creation except no Height Scale)
Material Panel: Change brush material; replace terrain material.
Bottom Functional Area: Area Actions, Water Body Actions, Path Operation.
VII. Shortcuts
Activation Requirements
Available in any Edit Mode or when the toolbar is in Area Actions mode.
Uniform Height (Alt key)
Hold down the Alt key. First click samples the area's layer count;
subsequent clicks apply that height to each valid area the brush touches.
Quick Generation (Shift key)
Hold down the Shift key, click to place a start area at the grid position,
hover to preview the end, then click the end to generate terrains.
Related Documentation:
AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
